2605.31049.title: Learning to Solve and Optimize by Evolving Code
2605.31049.paper_summary: Combinatorial and optimization problems are fundamental to many industrial AI applications. Solving large-scale real-world instances of such problems typically requires careful problem formalization, specialized solvers, and expert-designed heuristics. Thus, experts need to specify not only what solutions are, but also how they are derived. By introducing the tool CHECKMATE, we show that algorithm generation via code evolution represents a paradigm shift by eliminating the need to formulate the how. CHECKMATE solely relies on the what. Specifically, a formal specification ensures solutions' correctness and enables systematic performance evaluation of the generated programs, while a natural language description guides the evolutionary process. The effectiveness of our method is demonstrated on selected problems from two industrial domains: configuration and scheduling. In all cases, the evolved algorithms consistently outperform state-of-the-art solvers. This underscores the potential of formal methods in guiding code evolution for automatically solving complex real-world problems.
2605.31049.paper_summary_zh: 組合和優化問題對於許多工業 AI 應用來說是基礎性的。解決這類問題的大規模現實案例通常需要仔細的問題形式化、專門的求解器和專家設計的啟發式方法。因此，專家需要不僅指定解決方案是什麼，還要說明它們是如何得出的。通過引入工具 CHECKMATE，我們展示了通過代碼演化生成算法代表了一種範式轉變，因為它消除了對於如何形式化的需求。CHECKMATE 完全依賴於什麼。具體而言，正式規範確保了解決方案的正確性，並使生成程序的系統性能評估成為可能，而自然語言描述則指導演化過程。我們的方法在來自兩個工業領域的選定問題上得到了有效性證明：配置和排程。在所有情況下，演化的算法始終超越了最先進的求解器。這強調了正式方法在指導代碼演化以自動解決複雜現實問題方面的潛力。
2605.31049.author: Veronika Semmelrock et.al.
2605.31049.authors: Veronika Semmelrock, Benedetta Strizzolo, Francesco Zuccato, Gerhard Friedrich, Patrick Rodler, Konstantin Schekotihin
